- content: "Meeting notes\nWe discussed the new product features and the marketing\
    \ strategy in detail. The meeting was held in the main\nconference room and was\
    \ attended by the heads of the Development, Marketing, and Product teams. The\
    \ primary\nfocus was on enhancing the user experience and incorporating feedback\
    \ received from the initial market tests.\n\nAction items:\n  - Follow up with\
    \ the marketing team to finalize the go-to-market strategy. This includes setting\
    \ a timeline for the\n    campaign launch and identifying the key marketing channels.\n\
    \n  - Schedule a meeting with the development team to discuss the technical feasibility\
    \ of the proposed features.\n    It is crucial to assess the development timeline\
    \ and resource allocation.\n\n  - Update the product roadmap to reflect the new\
    \ features and timelines discussed. This updated roadmap\n    will be shared with\
    \ all stakeholders for transparency and to ensure alignment.\n\n  - Prepare a\
    \ competitive analysis report by analyzing the current market trends and competitor\
    \ products.\n    This will help in positioning our product effectively.\n\n  -\
    \ Organize a focus group session with select users to gather direct feedback on\
    \ the prototype.\n    This feedback will be invaluable in making the final adjustments\
    \ before the launch.\n\n  - Develop a contingency plan to address potential risks\
    \ identified during the meeting. This\n    includes supply chain disruptions,\
    \ technology challenges, and market entry barriers.\n\nThe next steps involve\
    \ each team working on their assigned tasks and reconvening in two weeks\nfor\
    \ a progress update. The goal is to launch the product by the end of the quarter,\
    \ and every\neffort will be made to adhere to this timeline.\n"

I can do that — before I send anything I need a couple quick clarifications so I email the right people and with the wording you want.

1) Who should receive these emails?
   - The file mentions teams (heads of Development, Marketing, Product) but no individual emails in the note itself. Do you want to:
     a) Email the team leads (Development, Marketing, Product)?  
     b) Email everyone who attended the meeting (if you want that I can try to find attendee emails in our calendar/contacts)?  
     c) Email the teams' distribution lists (e.g., dev@, marketing@) or specific people (please list addresses)?
2) Send as:
   - One group email to all recipients, or
   - Separate personalized emails per team/recipient?
3) Attach the meeting notes file (2024-05-08_product-meeting.docx, id 1) to the email(s)?
4) Any additional wording/tone or deadline you want included (I used the meeting's reconvene-in-two-weeks / launch-by-end-of-quarter info in the draft below — changeable)?

If you confirm (or give the recipient list), I will send the email(s). Below is a suggested subject and body you can approve or edit.

Suggested subject:
Action items from 2024-05-08 product meeting — next steps & owners

Suggested body:
Hi [Name / Team],

Thanks for attending the product meeting on 2024-05-08. Please see the action items assigned from the discussion and confirm you (or your team) own them and the expected delivery/timeline:

- Marketing: Finalize the go-to-market strategy — set campaign launch timeline and identify key marketing channels.
- Development: Schedule a meeting to review technical feasibility of the proposed features; assess development timeline and resource needs.
- Product: Update the product roadmap to reflect the new features and timelines; share the updated roadmap with stakeholders.
- Competitive/Market Research: Prepare a competitive analysis report based on current market trends and competitor products.
- UX/Research: Organize a focus group session with select users to gather direct feedback on the prototype.
- Ops/Risk: Develop a contingency plan addressing identified risks (supply chain, technology, market entry barriers).

Next steps: Please reply by [choose deadline] with (a) who will own each item, and (b) any blockers or resource needs. We will reconvene for a progress update in two weeks with the goal of staying on track for the end-of-quarter launch.

Thanks,
Emma Johnson
Blue Sparrow Tech

Tell me which recipients to use, whether to attach the notes, and any edits to the wording or deadlines — then I’ll send the email(s).
